label: Knowledge Tools
description: Knowledge Base API documentation with apply_json_patch operations, error handling, and file protection

architecture.label: Architecture
architecture.description: API-first design with JSON Patch operations

api.label: Json Patch
api.description:
RFC 6902 JSON Patch standard for describing modifications to JSON documents.

**File:** tools/apply_json_patch.py

**Standard:** RFC 6902

**Operations:**
- add - Insert or replace value
- remove - Delete value at path
- replace - Replace value at path
- move - Move value from one path to another
- copy - Copy value from one path to another
- test - Assert value equals expected before applying

operation_structure.op: Operation type (add, remove, replace, move, copy, test)

rendering.label: Document Rendering
rendering.description: Automatic markdown generation on patch application using pluggable RenderableModel classes. Handles file I/O for all model types (not a public API)

workflow.label: Workflow
workflow.description:
Complete knowledge tool operation workflow from API call to markdown generation.

**Main Flow:**
read JSON → validate patch → apply in memory → validate schema → write with protection → render markdown

**File Protection (within Write step):**
remove read-only → exclusive write → atomic rename → restore read-only

main_steps: 1. Read Document - Load and parse JSON file
2. Parse & Validate Patch - Validate RFC 6902 format
3. Apply in Memory - Execute patch operations on dict
4. Validate Schema - Check Pydantic Doc model
5. Write with Protection - Atomic file write with read-only management

file_protection.label: File Protection Purpose
file_protection.description: Files are protected with read-only attributes after writing to prevent accidental corruption or modification. This applies to both JSON knowledge documents and their auto-rendered markdown pairs.

purpose: Prevent accidental overwrites of validated JSON data
Ensure JSON and MD files always stay in sync (both locked together)
Protect against race conditions when multiple processes access files
Maintain document integrity as the source of truth for knowledge
file_locking_strategy: Both .json and .md files are set to read-only after successful write
Any modification requires removing read-only attribute first
Atomic write operations ensure files are never in partial state
Read-only flag restored immediately after write completes

testing.label: Testing
testing.description: Comprehensive test suite with 19 tests covering all functionality.

api.label: API
api.description: Public interfaces for knowledge tools: command-line scripts and Python functions

apply_json_patch_script.label: apply_json_patch.py
apply_json_patch_script.description: Apply JSON Patch operations to knowledge documents from command line with automatic markdown rendering. Supports both command-line arguments and stdin input to avoid shell escaping issues. Works with built-in models and pluggable custom models.

stdin_support.label: Using Stdin for Complex Patches
stdin_support.description: For complex JSON patches with many nested quotes, pass the patch via stdin to avoid shell escaping issues.

metadata.why: Shell escaping can be error-prone with deeply nested JSON. Use stdin for reliability.

functions.label: Functions
functions.description: Python function interfaces for programmatic use

apply_json_patch_func.label: apply_json_patch()
apply_json_patch_func.description: Apply JSON Patch to document file with validation and automatic markdown rendering.

parameters: document_path (str): Path to Doc JSON file
json_patch (Optional[str]): RFC 6902 JSON Patch operations as JSON string. If None, only re-renders without patching (default: None)
create (bool): If True, create document with patch as initial state (default: False)
external_models_path (Optional[str]): Path to folder with pluggable model definitions (default: None)
metadata.returns: None on success, ApplyPatchErrorResponse object on error with detailed context
metadata.behavior: All exceptions caught and returned as ApplyPatchErrorResponse with helpful hints
metadata.safety: Atomic operations, in-memory validation before write, file protection workflow, read-only file management
metadata.rendering: Every successful patch automatically generates markdown: document.json → document.md with identical file protection
metadata.code: apply_json_patch(document_path: str, json_patch: Optional[str] = None, create: bool = False, external_models_path: Optional[str] = None) -> Optional[ApplyPatchErrorResponse]

rendering_behavior.label: Rendering Behavior
rendering_behavior.description: When apply_json_patch succeeds, it always attempts to regenerate the markdown file.

when_renders: After successful patch application and write
When called with no patch (re-render only mode)
In both --stdin and command-line argument modes
what_happens: 1. JSON is validated against Pydantic schema
2. File is written with atomic operations
3. Markdown (.md file) is automatically generated from the JSON
4. Both files are set to read-only for protection
rendering_failures: If rendering fails, operation still succeeds (JSON is safe)
User is warned via stderr: ⚠️ Warning: Failed to render markdown: ...
JSON and markdown may be out of sync
Patch command will exit with code 0 (success)
metadata.important: Always check stderr for warnings about rendering failures

pluggable_models.label: Pluggable Models
pluggable_models.description: Extend apply_json_patch with custom model types by loading external model definitions from a folder.

overview.label: Overview
overview.description: Pluggable models allow you to define custom document types beyond the built-in Doc model. External models are loaded dynamically and work seamlessly with apply_json_patch validation and rendering.

key_points: Load custom model classes from external Python files
All internal models (Doc, Task, etc.) continue to work normally
External and internal models coexist in the same registry
Custom models inherit from RenderableModel and implement render()
Validation works the same way as built-in models
internal_models: Doc - Default document model with hierarchical children
Task - Optional task tracking model (if available)

usage.label: Usage
usage.description: How to use pluggable models with apply_json_patch.

metadata.folder_structure:
custom_models/
  my_model.py      # Contains class MyModel(RenderableModel)
  other_model.py   # Contains class OtherModel(RenderableModel)

creating_models.label: Creating Custom Models
creating_models.description: How to create a pluggable model.

requirements: Implement render() method returning markdown string
Use Pydantic Field for schema validation
metadata.example_code:
from typing import Literal
from pydantic import Field
from models import RenderableModel

class MyModel(RenderableModel):
    type: Literal["MyModel"] = "MyModel"
    id: str = Field(..., description="Unique ID")
    title: str = Field(..., description="Title")
    
    def render(self) -> str:
        return f"# {self.title}"
    
    def tips(self) -> list:
        return ["Custom model loaded successfully"]

opts_configuration.label: Opts Configuration
opts_configuration.description: Non-displayable rendering options for document nodes

render_priority_field.label: render_priority Field
render_priority_field.description: When true, renders node before siblings with render_priority=false

file_modification_workflow.label: File Modification Workflow
file_modification_workflow.description: How to properly modify knowledge documents using the tool.

metadata.important: ALWAYS use apply_json_patch to modify documents. Never edit JSON files directly.
workflow: 2. Call apply_json_patch with document path and patch
3. Tool automatically: removes read-only → writes atomically → restores read-only → renders markdown
why_important: Ensures data consistency and validation through Pydantic schema
Provides error handling and helpful hints
metadata.wrong_way: ❌ Directly editing knowledge_tool.json with a text editor or file tools
